trade.summary: France is Spain's largest trading partner. Trade is deeply integrated through the EU single market. Spain exports vehicles, fruits and vegetables, fuels, and chemical products; France exports aircraft, vehicles, pharmaceuticals, and electrical equipment. The Pyrenean corridor is a critical EU logistics artery.
disputes: Pyrenean rail bottleneck limiting trade flow capacity

diplomatic.status: Close EU partners and bilateral allies
diplomatic.summary: Spain and France are close EU partners with deep bilateral ties. The 2023 Barcelona Treaty updated and deepened the bilateral relationship, similar to France's Aachen Treaty with Germany. Both cooperate on Mediterranean policy, EU governance, and climate. Occasional tensions over fisheries and regional identity (Catalan independence movement seeking French sympathy) are managed diplomatically.

military.summary: Both NATO members with deep military cooperation. France and Spain cooperate extensively in the Sahel (Operation Barkhane successor operations), on EU defense initiatives (PESCO), and on Eurofighter and Airbus military programs. Joint exercises are frequent.

financial.summary: France is among Spain's largest investors. French banks (BNP Paribas, Crédit Agricole) and utilities (Engie, Veolia) have major Spain presences. Spanish firms (Inditex, Santander, Iberdrola) have significant French operations. Shared eurozone membership deepens financial integration.

energy.summary: Spain is a major LNG import hub and has been pushing for better electricity and gas interconnections with France (the Pyrenean bottleneck). The MidCat gas pipeline project (now recast as the BarMar hydrogen corridor) seeks to route Iberian energy to the rest of Europe via France. Electricity interconnection capacity is being expanded.

scientific.summary: Extensive scientific cooperation through EU Horizon programs. France and Spain co-author prolifically across all scientific disciplines. Strong academic exchange; many Spanish researchers work at French institutions and vice versa. Shared interests in Mediterranean, marine, and climate sciences.
